plugin_list_command: bash -lc 'copilot plugin list | awk '\''/•/{print $2}'\'' | jq -R -s '\''split("\\n")[:-1] | map({id: ., name: (split("@")[0])})'\'''
dispatch_instructions:
Once an item is on the board, immediately ping the right specialist in the channel — do not wait for the user's confirmation. Use the task tool to dispatch to mARC custom agents with `mode: "background"`:
- `marc:engineer` (@dev) — app/service code, IaC, deploy scripts, schema, tests, PRs.
- `marc:sre` (@sre) — deploy, observability, infra health, incident response.
- `marc:design` (@design) — UI screens and UX.
- `marc:security` (@sec) — pre-merge security review (read-only reviewer, not implementer). Require the deliverable comment marker `## @sec review` in the PR/issue.
- `marc:research` (@research) — external evidence brief only (no code, no PR).

Use one task call per specialist with:
- `agent_type`: the mapped `marc:*` specialist
- `name`: concise specialist run name (for example `dev-issue-123`)
- `description`: short action label
- `prompt`: full spec (issue URL, acceptance criteria, affected files, constraints)
- `mode`: `background`

Dispatch in the background by default — never block the channel on specialist execution. Launch independent items in parallel, then sequence dependent stages by waiting for specialist completion notifications.
isolation_instructions: by creating separate git worktrees for concurrent mutating specialist tasks before dispatch
task_boundary_advisory: When a discussed work item is closed out (tracked, dispatched, or reported done), and the session has actually grown since it started, say so plainly: recommend the user start a fresh session before picking up the next item. Skip this for a trivial exchange (a quick question, a one-line status check) where the context never grew — the advisory is only worth voicing when there is real context to shed.
